name: Luxe Bay-Scallop Stew
recipeIngredient: 6 Garlic cloves, peeled and sliced thin
3 tablespoons Wine Vinegar (Champagne preferred, more to taste)
2 ounces French Green Beans, blanched and cut into 1-1/4" pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. Tie fresh basil, dill, and tarragon in cheesecloth. Save a little extra for garnish.
2. Heat olive oil over low heat in a large saucepan with carrot, onion, and fennel. Stir occasionally until vegetables are softened but not brown. Add leek, garlic, and saffron; cook and stir until soft. Add fish broth and herb sachet. Bring both to a simmer, then add vinegar and season to taste with salt and a pinch of cayenne. Strain 2 cups of broth into a small saucepan. Reserve the remaining broth and vegetables.
3. Meanwhile, heat water in a small saucepan. When it boils, add 1 tablespoon salt and green beans. When just tender, transfer them to a bowl of cold water with a slotted spoon. Add the diced potato and cook until just tender. Drain. Prepare tomato (remove seeds).
4. To finish the dish, divide the green beans, potato, and tomato among six soup bowls or plates. Reheat the broth and vegetables. Heat broth in a small pan. When it simmers, add the scallops. Cook only a minute or two, then portion out the vegetables and broth. Add scallops and their broth. Garnish with a sprinkling of coarsely chopped basil, dill, and tarragon. Serve immediately.
